title: Mean value estimates for Weyl sums in two dimensions

thm:1.1.summary: Decoupling for the two-dimensional Parsell–Vinogradov surface $\mathcal{M}_{2,k}=\{(t,s,t^2,ts,s^2,\dots):(t,s)\in[0,1]^2\}\subset\mathbb{R}^{k(k+3)/2}$: $D_k(N,p)\lesssim_{\epsilon,p}N^{\cdots+\epsilon}$, sharp and unconditional for $k=2$ (all $p$) and $k=3$ ($2\le p\le16$), and conditionally sharp for higher $k$. The 2D analogue of moment-curve decoupling — yields mean value estimates for 2D Weyl sums.

conj:1.2.summary: The full decoupling conjecture for $\mathcal{M}_{2,k}$: $D_k(N,p)\lesssim_\epsilon N^{\frac2k(\frac12-\frac1p)+\epsilon}$ for $2\le p\le k(k+3)-2$. The target; the paper proves it for $k=2,3$ and reduces the general case to a curvature/non-degeneracy conjecture.

thm:2.1.summary: Number-theoretic consequence: the 2D Parsell–Vinogradov mean value $\tilde J_{s,2,k}(X)\lesssim_\epsilon X^\epsilon(X^{2s}+X^{4s-\dim})$ in the optimal ranges (unconditional for $k=2$). The Vinogradov-type counting bound for systems indexed by $(\mathbb{Z}^2)$.

thm:2.2.summary: Exponential-sum form: a sharp $L^p$ mean value for the 2D Weyl sum $\sum_i a_i e(x\cdot\Phi_k(t_i,s_i))$ over $\delta$-separated points on the surface, with $R\gtrsim N^2$. The discrete-restriction form of thm:1.1.

cor:2.4.summary: Solution count for a coupled system of two linear equations plus two near-equal fourth-power-sum constraints (a 2D Diophantine system) — an explicit number-theoretic payoff of the $k=4$-flavoured estimate.

thm:3.1.summary: Brascamp–Lieb finiteness criterion: $\sup_{f_j}\frac{|\Lambda(f_1,\dots,f_m)|}{\prod_j\|f_j\|_{L^{p_j}}}<\infty$ iff $n=\sum_j n_j/p_j$ and the subspace dimension condition holds. The external multilinear-geometry input.

thm:3.2.summary: The $L^n$ / multilinear-Kakeya form of Brascamp–Lieb: $\sup_{g_j}\frac{\|(\prod_j g_j\circ\pi_j)^{1/m}\|_{L^n}}{(\prod_j\|g_j\|_{L^2})^{1/m}}<\infty$ iff $\dim(V)\le\frac{n}{2m}\sum_j\dim(\pi_j(V))$ for all $V$. The geometric estimate the multilinear restriction is built on.

lem:4.1.summary: Non-degeneracy of the quadratic part: for $k=2$ there is no $3$-dimensional $V\subset\mathbb{R}^5$ on which all the associated quadratic forms $Q_{v,w}$ vanish (and analogously for $k=3$ in $\mathbb{R}^9$). Ensures the surface is genuinely curved in the right sense.

conj:4.2.summary: The general curvature/non-degeneracy conjecture for $\mathcal{M}_{2,k}$ ($k\ge2$): no subspace on which all the quadratic forms degenerate. Verified for $k=2,3$; assumed for higher $k$ — the single conditional hypothesis behind thm:1.1's higher-$k$ part.

def:4.3.summary: A collection of $m\ge n$ sets $S_j\subset[0,1]^2$ is $\nu$-transverse for $\mathcal{M}_{2,k}$ if every sub-collection of the relevant size spans with parallelepiped volume $>\nu$. The transversality hypothesis adapted to the 2D surface.

prop:4.4.summary: Assuming the non-degeneracy conjecture: $\nu$-transverse points $(t_j,s_j)$ give tangent planes $V_j$ satisfying the Brascamp–Lieb dimension condition. Bridges geometric transversality of points to the analytic hypothesis of thm:3.2.

thm:4.5.summary: Pigeonholing transversality: there is $\Lambda_k$ such that for each $K$ there is $\nu_K>0$ with the property that any $\Lambda K$ or more squares in a $K$-column $\mathbf{Col}_K$ are $\nu_K$-transverse for $\mathcal{M}_{2,k}$. Lets the broad/narrow split always produce a transverse sub-family.

thm:5.1.summary: The main multilinear restriction inequality: assuming the non-degeneracy conjecture, for $\Lambda K$ pairwise distinct squares and $f_j$ supported near $\mathcal{N}_{R_j,1/N}$, $\|(\prod_jE_{R_j}g_j)^{1/m}\|_{L^r(B_N)}\lesssim_\epsilon N^\epsilon\cdots$. The transverse multilinear estimate the decoupling is bootstrapped from.

thm:6.3.summary: Multilinear decoupling from the restriction estimate (assuming the ansatz of §6): $\|(\prod_jE_{R_j}g_j)^{1/m}\|_{L^r(B_N)}\lesssim N^\epsilon\cdots$ in square-function form. Multilinear restriction upgraded to a decoupling statement.

cor:6.4.summary: Ball-inflation form of thm:6.3: relates the $N^{-1/2}$-scale transverse square-function product to the $N^{-1}$-scale one, the building block of the iteration.

prop:7.1.summary: Rescaling: for a square $R$ of side $N^{-\rho}$ with $\rho\le1/k$ and a ball $B_N\subset\mathbb{R}^{k(k+3)/2}$, $\|E_R^{(k)}g\|_{L^p(w_{B_N})}\lesssim D_k(N^{1-\cdots},p)\cdots$. The affine-invariance step for the 2D surface.

thm:8.1.summary: Linear-versus-multilinear bootstrap: there is $\beta(K,p)\to0$ as $K\to\infty$ with $D_k(N,p)\le N^{\beta(K,p)+\frac2k(\frac12-\frac1p)}\cdots$ for $N\ge K$. Reduces the linear decoupling constant to the transverse multilinear one (via column decomposition).

lem:8.2.summary: Flat ($l^p$-orthogonality) decoupling: for $K^{-1}$-squares $R_i$, $\|\sum_iE_{R_i}^{(k)}g\|_{L^p(w_{B_K})}\lesssim_p M^{1-2/p}(\sum_i\|E_{R_i}^{(k)}g\|_{L^p}^p)^{1/p}$. The cheap decoupling at the coarse scale, used in the broad/narrow accounting.

prop:8.4.summary: Broad/narrow (column) inequality: $\|E_R^{(k)}g\|_{L^p(w_{B_N})}^p\le C_pK^{p-2}\sum_{R'\subset R,\,R'\in\mathbf{Col}_K}\|E_{R'}^{(k)}g\|^p+\cdots$ for $N>\delta^{-k}K$. The dichotomy split into a $K$-column sum plus a transverse remainder.

thm:9.1.summary: Proof of the main theorem: assuming the non-degeneracy conjecture for some $k\ge2$, $D_k(N,p)\lesssim_\epsilon N^{\frac2k(\frac12-\frac1p)+\epsilon}$ for $2\le p\le k(k+3)-2$. Combining the bootstrap thm:8.1 with the iterated ball-inflation closes the induction; unconditional for $k=2,3$ via lem:4.1.

lem:9.2.summary: Iterated ball-inflation: for $\Lambda K$ transverse squares and $s\ge2$, $\|(\prod_i|E_{R_i}^{(k)}g_i|)^{1/(\Lambda K)}\|_{L^p(w_{B_N})}\le N^{2^{-s}}\|(\prod_i\sum_{l(\Delta)=N^{-2^{-s}}}|E_\Delta g_i|^2)^{1/(\cdots)}\|$. Iterating cor:6.4 sends the loss to $1$ — the induction-on-scales engine.

edges.note: thm:1.1 is thm:9.1 made unconditional for $k=2,3$ (the non-degeneracy hypothesis being verified there).
For $k=2,3$ the curvature/non-degeneracy conjecture is established directly.
The linear decoupling constant is bootstrapped down to $N^\epsilon$ by iterating the linear-vs-multilinear inequality.
The transverse multilinear term is controlled by the iterated ball-inflation, which sends the loss to $1$.
The higher-$k$ statement is conditional on the non-degeneracy conjecture for $\mathcal{M}_{2,k}$.
Establishes the decoupling conjecture for $\mathcal{M}_{2,k}$ unconditionally for $k=2,3$ and conditionally otherwise.
The reduction runs through the quantitative broad/narrow (column) inequality.
The flat decoupling handles the coarse-scale orthogonality in the accounting.
Sub-squares are rescaled to unit scale between iteration steps.
Pigeonholing guarantees the narrow term contains a $\nu$-transverse sub-family of squares.
Iterates the single ball-inflation cycle $s$ times.
Ball-inflation form of the multilinear decoupling.
Multilinear decoupling is bootstrapped from the multilinear restriction estimate.
The multilinear restriction estimate is built on the $L^n$ Brascamp–Lieb / multilinear-Kakeya inequality.
Transverse points are converted to tangent planes satisfying the Brascamp–Lieb dimension condition.
The $L^n$ form is the relevant special case of the general Brascamp–Lieb finiteness criterion.
Uses the non-degeneracy of the quadratic part (verified for $k=2,3$; conjectural otherwise).
For $k\ge4$ the implication relies on the non-degeneracy conjecture.
Shows large sub-collections of column squares are $\nu$-transverse in the sense of def:4.3.
The 2D Parsell–Vinogradov mean value bound is read off from the decoupling estimate.
The 2D Weyl-sum mean value is the discrete-restriction form of decoupling.
The coupled Diophantine-system count follows from the mean value estimate.

main_results.description: Decoupling for the 2D Parsell–Vinogradov surface and its number-theoretic consequences (Weyl-sum and Diophantine-system counts).

brascamp_lieb_geometry.description: The Brascamp–Lieb finiteness criterion, its $L^n$ multilinear-Kakeya form, the non-degeneracy of the surface's quadratic part, and the transversality definitions/pigeonholing.

multilinear_restriction_and_decoupling.description: The transverse multilinear restriction estimate, its upgrade to multilinear decoupling, and the ball-inflation form.

rescaling_and_bootstrap.description: Rescaling, the flat decoupling, the broad/narrow column dichotomy, the linear-vs-multilinear bootstrap, the iterated ball-inflation, and the final proof closing the induction.

BD-l2.title: The proof of the l^2 Decoupling Conjecture

BDG.title: Proof of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem for degrees higher than three

BCCT:BrascampLieb.title: Finite bounds for Hölder-Brascamp-Lieb multilinear inequalities

ParsellPrendivilleWooley.title: Near-optimal mean value estimates for multidimensional Weyl sums
